interpretation: Fair — significant data quality issues detected

recommended_actions.action: Investigate duplicate records. Add dedup logic keyed on non-key fields.
Fix null values in required column 'user_id' at the source.
Investigate root cause of active_users discrepancy for 2025-12. Check for duplicate events, missing data, or filter logic differences.
Investigate root cause of churn_rate discrepancy for 2025-12. Check for duplicate events, missing data, or filter logic differences.

top_risks.business_impact: Duplicate records inflate aggregations and distort metrics
top_risks.title: Near-duplicate rows detected

top_risks.business_impact: Null values in required fields break filters and aggregations
top_risks.title: Null values in required column: user_id

top_risks.business_impact: Reported active_users for 2025-12 is 1,794, but recomputed value from raw events is 9,583. Discrepancy of -434.2%.
top_risks.title: active_users misreported for 2025-12 (off by -434.2%)

top_risks.business_impact: Reported churn_rate for 2025-12 is 30.6%, but recomputed value from raw events is 4.8%. Discrepancy of +84.4%.
top_risks.title: churn_rate misreported for 2025-12 (off by +84.4%)

executive_summary: BayesIQ audits a SaaS company's core reporting pipeline covering active users, churn rate, and MRR calculations. The audit surfaces 4 critical issues — including a 434% active user undercount caused by deduplication failures and a churn rate overstated by 84% due to denominator drift. Leadership had been making expansion and retention decisions based on fundamentally wrong numbers. After remediation, the reliability score improves from 31 to 64.
